ip_outcome_note: Card name = species name = canonical Pokemon species. PokemonTCG.io confirms nationalPokedexNumbers: [353], species 'Shuppet'. Auto-verify applies.

trivia_bullets.text: Vivid Voltage (SWSH4) released November 13, 2020. Shuppet appears as a Basic Psychic-type Common (067/185). Regulation Mark D. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh4]

Shuppet is National Pokedex no. 353, introduced in Generation III (Hoenn). Its design is based on the teru teru bozu — a traditional Japanese paper doll hung to wish for sunny weather — and on tsukumogami mythology (inanimate objects gaining spirit). Its Japanese name, Kagebouzu, combines kage (shadow) and teru teru bozu. It evolves into Banette starting at level 37. [WebSearch snippet: Bulbapedia/Shuppet; Bulbanews 'On the Origin of Species: Shuppet and Banette']
sources: Bulbanews: On the Origin of Species: Shuppet and Banette

trivia_bullets.text: "There's a proverb that says, 'Shun the house where Shuppet gather in the growing dusk.'" [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh4-67]

Canonically, Shuppet feeds on negative emotions — grudges, jealousy, and sorrow — and congregates near homes where people harbor long-held resentments. Its evolution Banette originates from a discarded plush doll filled with hatred, animated by a spirit seeking revenge on the child who abandoned it. The doll-to-doll pipeline makes this one of the more coherent two-stage lore progressions in Gen 3. [WebSearch snippet: Bulbapedia/Banette; bogleech.com/pokemon/p353shuppet]

This printing: Basic Psychic-type, 60 HP, weak to Darkness (x2), resistant to Fighting (-30). Single attack: Haunt (1 Psychic Energy) — places 1 damage counter on the opponent's Active Pokemon. Artist: Miki Tanaka. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh4-67]
